predictions.text: By 2026-07-31, Meta will report total headcount at 2026-06-30 at least 8% lower than headcount reported for 2026-03-31, as shown in Meta’s Q2 2026 Form 10-Q and Q1 2026 Form 10-Q (or the earnings release if headcount is disclosed there).
predictions.verification_criteria: Meta 2026 Q2 10-Q reports employees at 2026-06-30 and Meta 2026 Q1 10-Q reports employees at 2026-03-31; Q2 value <= 0.92 * Q1 value.
monitoring_signals: Meta earnings date and 10-Q release
Public reporting of restructuring/layoff rounds at Meta
WARN notices in key U.S. states (e.g., CA) mentioning Meta Platforms
predictions.rationale: Reported plans for ~10% cuts suggest a measurable near-term headcount decline, but timing and scope could be staggered across quarters.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, Cerebras Systems will complete a U.S. IPO (NYSE or Nasdaq) with first day of regular-way trading, and the base deal (excluding greenshoe) will raise at least $300 million in gross proceeds as shown in the final prospectus (424B).
predictions.verification_criteria: Cerebras begins trading on NYSE/Nasdaq following an effective S-1; final prospectus shows gross proceeds (base deal) >= $300M.
monitoring_signals: SEC EDGAR: amended S-1 filings, effectiveness notice, 424B prospectus
Exchange listing announcements
IPO calendar/underwriter syndicate updates
predictions.rationale: Refiling signals intent, but AI-IPO windows can shut quickly if risk sentiment deteriorates.

predictions.text: By 2026-10-31, the White House (or OMB) will issue a binding federal directive (Executive Order, OMB memorandum, or equivalent) requiring U.S. federal agencies to apply specific pre-deployment testing/red-teaming requirements to “frontier” generative AI models, where “frontier” is defined in the directive by an explicit compute or capability threshold.
predictions.verification_criteria: Officially published EO/OMB memo includes (i) mandatory requirements (not voluntary guidance), (ii) applies to GenAI/frontier models, and (iii) defines frontier via an explicit threshold (compute/capability) plus required testing/red-teaming steps.
monitoring_signals: White House/OMB AI policy releases
NIST or Commerce references to federal compliance obligations
Agency procurement policy updates citing frontier-model evaluations
predictions.rationale: Active policy negotiations increase odds of a concrete federal standard, but industry pushback could keep measures voluntary or non-thresholded.

predictions.text: By 2026-09-30, CISA will announce a new publicly accessible vulnerability-context program (product, service, or formal partnership) that provides enrichment/metadata for at least 50% of newly published CVEs, with a documented API or machine-readable feed.
predictions.verification_criteria: CISA publishes an announcement plus documentation for an API/feed offering enrichment; stated coverage target or measured coverage indicates >=50% of newly published CVEs enriched.
monitoring_signals: CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) updates expanding scope
Budget/contract awards for vulnerability data services
Public API/documentation releases on cisa.gov or GitHub
predictions.rationale: If NVD enrichment degrades, pressure rises for CISA to fill the operational gap for prioritization, but coverage commitments may be limited.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, the CFTC or DOJ will publicly announce at least one enforcement action (civil complaint, criminal charges, or settlement) involving alleged market manipulation/insider trading linked to crude oil or refined-product trading during the April 2026 Iran conflict period.
predictions.verification_criteria: CFTC/DOJ press release or court filing names at least one defendant/respondent and explicitly links the conduct to trading during the April 2026 Iran-conflict window (or references the conflict as the event context).
monitoring_signals: CFTC enforcement press releases mentioning crude/oil products
DOJ/USAO indictments referencing oil futures/options
Congressional letters/referrals to CFTC/DOJ becoming public
predictions.rationale: Congressional scrutiny can catalyze enforcement, but proving intent and building cases often takes longer than a year.

predictions.text: By 2026-06-30, at least one major shipping analytics provider (e.g., Kpler, Vortexa, MarineTraffic, Lloyd’s List Intelligence) will publish an article/note stating that Strait of Hormuz crude-oil tanker transits have returned to at least 90% of their pre-conflict baseline on a 14-day rolling basis.
predictions.verification_criteria: A publicly accessible report explicitly claims 14-day rolling crude tanker transits through Hormuz are >=90% of a defined pre-conflict baseline (e.g., February–March 2026 average).
monitoring_signals: Daily AIS-based transit counts from shipping analytics dashboards
War-risk premium and security-advisory changes for Hormuz
Announcements by major tanker operators on routing normalizations
predictions.rationale: ‘Open’ declarations often precede gradual normalization; a 90% threshold is plausible if no new attacks occur.

predictions.text: At the 2026-09-16 FOMC meeting, the Federal Reserve will keep the target range for the federal funds rate unchanged from the range in effect on 2026-04-18 (no cut and no hike), as shown in the official FOMC statement.
predictions.verification_criteria: Compare the federal funds target range in the 2026-09-16 FOMC statement to the target range in effect on 2026-04-18; they must match exactly.
monitoring_signals: CPI/PCE inflation prints and revisions
Monthly payrolls/unemployment rate surprises
Fed speakers shifting from ‘hold’ to explicit easing/tightening bias
predictions.rationale: Two-sided inflation and growth risks favor policy inertia, but a sharp inflation or labor shock could force a move.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, the Government of Canada will table federal legislation to expand national-security review/screening for foreign investment in at least one explicitly named strategic sector (e.g., critical minerals, semiconductors/AI compute infrastructure, telecom/satcom), as shown by a bill introduced in Parliament and a corresponding government news release/backgrounder.
predictions.verification_criteria: A Canadian federal bill is introduced (House of Commons) that amends the Investment Canada Act (or equivalent) to expand national-security screening, and the government explicitly cites at least one strategic sector in the bill text or official release.
monitoring_signals: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada (ISED) announcements
Parliament bill introductions/amendments to the Investment Canada Act
High-profile blocked/conditioned foreign investment cases
predictions.rationale: Greater geopolitical friction and ‘diversify from the U.S.’ messaging make tighter screening plausible, but legislative bandwidth and political tradeoffs may delay action.
